title: delivery worker holds circuit-breaker Mutex lock while doing DB work
description: In process_batch, the circuit-breaker Mutex is locked with br.is_tripped() inside an explicit block, and separately locked again with breaker.lock().await.record_success/failure after each HTTP result. The lock is not held during the await, which is correct. However record_success and record_failure also call Instant::now() inside the lock. This is minor but: the success path does breaker.lock().await after a sqlx DELETE also inside the same loop iteration, meaning the lock is acquired twice per successful delivery. These could be merged. Also is_tripped() takes &mut self but only reads — it removes an entry when the cooldown expires, which makes it silently mutate on what looks like a read call.
close_reason: False positive: mutex lock is already scoped in a block (lines 179-185), released before DB/network work.
